memoryArtifacts.headline: George Stronge of Chard…"A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry" Vol II p 1333
memoryArtifacts.description:
George Stronge of Chard…"A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry" Vol II p 1333: Brief Extraction page 1333: 
George Stronge, of Chard, co. Somerset, 8 Elizabeth, seems to have been engaged in serge manufatures, the staple of those parts and had issue, Thomas and Walter. The elder son,
     Thomas Stronge, of Chardstoke, left a son,
      James Stronge, b. 1619; he became rector of Bettiscombe and vicar of Ilminster; he m. 1s, Miss Minterne, niece of the equestrian family of Brown of Frampton, and had by her a son, Melancthon. By a 2nd wife he had further issue, and particularly Martin, b. 1663, thirty years vicar of Yeovil, m. a dau of Phelips of Montacute, and left issue.
     Melancthon Stronge, the elder son, b. 1650, describes himself as of Nether-Stronge, and appears to have allowed himself to be drawn in to take pars (part) in the unfortunate rising and defeat of James, Duke of Monmouth. Immediately after the battle of Sedgemoor, near Bridgewater, on the 4th July, 1685, and the disastrous defeat which followed, he fled away on foot, disguised in the garb of a working artizan, with his tools, etc., to London, which he reached in great misery. Sometime previous to this adventure and happy escape from the massacre and from the horrors of the "Bloddy Assize," he had m. and had (with two daus., Mary, wif of Gosforth, and Elizabeth, who m. Samuel Sandeforth, Esq., and had a dau., Anne, m. 12 Apr, 1742, to Robert Streatfeild, Esq.) a son,
      Melancthon Stronge, who settled at Garratt, Surrey, and d. 28 Mar 1750, leaving a son,
     Melancthon Strong, b. 1706; m. Eleanor, dau of Thomas Sanders, Esq. of Hookwood, in Charlwood (of the ancient family of Sanders of Sanderstead) and had issue...(See Below and continued on page 1334)
Note: This source has some errors.

popularStory.description: When Thomas Strong and his twin brother Walter were born in 1584 in South Chard, Somerset, England, their father, George, was 24, and their mother, Elizabeth, was 23. He married Joanna Bagge on May 12 …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Strong於1579年出生在英國薩默塞特素斯 哈德時，他的父親George Strong23歲，母親Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond20歲。他於1603年5月12日與Joanna Bagge在英國戴文哈德斯托可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1663年5月在英國多瑟特哈德斯托可享年84歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭多瑟特哈德斯托可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Strong于1579年出生在英国薩默塞特素斯 哈德时，他的父亲George Strong23岁，母亲Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond20岁。他于1603年5月12日与Joanna Bagge在英国戴文哈德斯托可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他去世于1663年5月在英国多瑟特哈德斯托可享年84岁，埋葬在英国英格兰多瑟特哈德斯托可。

nameMeanings.name: Strong
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English strong(e), strang(e) ‘strong, powerful’ (Old English strang).
Americanized form (translation into English) of Jewish   Stark  .

variants.name: Strang
meanings: German: topographic name from Middle High German stranc ‘strip of land’, or a habitational name from any of the places so called.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a rope maker, from Middle High German stranc, German Strang ‘cord, rope’.

variants.name: Trehan
meanings: Indian: Sikh name, from a name of a sub-caste of Khatris.

Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name, a variant of French Trahand, from a derivative of French traire ‘to draw or stretch’, for a silk worker whose job was to draw out the thread from cocoons. In Britain it has sometimes been ‘translated’ into English as Strong .

variants.name: Trahan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Monique, Pierre, Leonce, Lucien, Alcide, Cecile, Emile, Adrien, Camille.

French: variant of Trahant or Trahand (and, in North America, an altered form of the former), a metonymic occupational name for a silkworker who drew out the thread from the cocoons, from a derivative of the verb traire ‘to draw or stretch’. Compare Strong .

History: The LA branches of the Trahan family trace their descent from Guillaume Trahan, from Montreuil-Bellay in Maine-et-Loire, who arrived in Acadia in the 17th century; he married Madeleine Brun in Port-Royal in Acadia (now Annapolis Royal in NS, Canada), c. 1666. At the time of the expulsion of the Acadians (1755–64), his numerous descendants were deported and finally settled principally in the Attakapas country of LA.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Strong was born in 1579, in South Chard, Somerset, England, his father, George Strong, was 23 and his mother, Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond, was 20. He married Joanna Bagge on 12 May 1603, in Chardstock, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 8 daughters. He died in May 1663, in Chardstock, Dorset, England, at the age of 84, and was buried in Chardstock, Dorset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Strong est né en 1579, à South Chard, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, George Strong, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Joanna Bagge le 12 mai 1603, à Chardstock, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé en mai 1663, à Chardstock, Dorset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterré à Chardstock, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Strong 1579 in South Chard, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater George Strong 23 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond 20. Er hat am 12. Mai 1603 in Chardstock, Devon, England Joanna Bagge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Er starb im Mai 1663 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Chardstock, Dorset, England und wurde in Chardstock, Dorset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Strong è nato il nel 1579 nel South Chard, Somerset, England, il suo padre, George Strong, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Joanna Bagge il 12 maggio 1603, nel Chardstock, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 8 figlie. Morì nel maggio 1663, nel Chardstock, Dorset, England, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolto a Chardstock, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Strongが1579年にイギリスソメルセトソウス チャードで生まれた時，父George Strongは23歳，母Elizabeth Mary Ann Bondは20歳であった。1603年5月12日にイギリスデボンチャードストックでJoanna Baggeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1663年5月にイギリスドールセトチャードストックで84歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスドールセトチャードストックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Strong(이)가 1579년 영국 서머세트 소우스 처드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Strong(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1603년 5월 12일 영국 데폰 처스톡에서 Joanna Bagge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1663년 5월 영국 도르세트 처스톡에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 처스톡에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Strong nasceu em 1579, em South Chard, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, George Strong, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Joanna Bagge em 12 de maio de 1603, em Chardstock, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu em maio de 1663, em Chardstock, Dorset, Inglaterra, com 84 anos, e foi sepultado em Chardstock, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Strong родился в 1579 г. в местности соут чард, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени George Strong было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Joanna Bagge 12 мая 1603 г. в местности чардстокк, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 8 дочерей. Он умер май 1663 г. в местности чардстокк, дорсет, Англия в возрасте 84 лет и был похоронен в местности чардстокк, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Strong nació en 1579, en South Chard, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, George Strong, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Joanna Bagge el 12 de mayo de 1603, en Chardstock, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió en mayo de 1663, en Chardstock, Dorset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultado en Chardstock, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
